title: Bridge Essay: Habit learning and memory in mammals: behavioral and neural characteristics.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.

**Habit learning and memory in mammals: behavioral and neural characteristics.**. Neurobiol Learn Mem.. PMID: 24981854. DOI: 10.1016/j.nlm.2014.06.010. Goal-direct behavior and habit learning represent two forms of instrumental learning; whereas the former is rapidly acquired and regulated by its outcome, the latter is reflexive, elicited by antecedent stimuli rather than their consequences. Habit learning can be generally defined as the acquisition of associations between stimuli and responses.
